title: 10 best herbal teas for toothache

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with toothache because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active ingredients, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain by combating the underlying bacterial infection. Additionally, pot marigold's astringent properties can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding in the gums, promoting a faster recovery from tooth-related issues. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from toothache symptoms without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea's antimicrobial compounds can help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that may be contributing to tooth pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in pot marigold tea may reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with toothaches.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea's antioxidants can help protect against oral infections and inflammation, promoting overall oral health and potentially reducing the severity of toothache symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them a popular choice for toothache relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the flowers by spreading them on paper towels and allowing air circulation for 2 hours.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers with boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a fine-mesh sieve into another cup.
Drink the tea immediately while warm, or refrigerate for up to 24 hours before consuming.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the affected area. The tea's antibacterial compounds also help to combat any infection or inflammation in the mouth, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's warming effect can help to increase blood flow to the gums, promoting healing and relaxation. By drinking herbal ginger teas, you can experience relief from toothache symptoms and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compound, gingerol, helps reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothache.
Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial compounds in ginger tea, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help combat bacterial infections that may be causing toothache and promote healing.

Analgesic property: Ginger's analgesic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of compounds like gingerol and sesquiterpenes, can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with toothache.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Known as "Ginger's main event", rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in teeth and gums, making them a popular choice for toothache relief.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and natural analgesics, ginger roots help to ease toothaches by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that can cause infections.
remedy_recipe: Rinse and peel one-inch piece of fresh ginger root weighing about 10 grams.
Slice the peeled ginger into thin coins about 1/4 inch thick for maximum extraction.
Boil one cup of filtered water in a medium-sized pot for 5-7 minutes.
Add the sliced ginger to the boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large mug.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender teas helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's calming effects can help reduce pain and swelling in the mouth, providing relief from the discomfort associated with a toothache. Additionally, lavender's antibacterial properties may also help combat infections that contribute to toothaches, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lavender tea's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender tea, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lavender tea's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress that may exacerbate toothache symptoms, promoting relaxation and reducing overall discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in soothing toothaches.
Seeds: Containing essential oils with antibacterial properties, they help combat oral infections and reduce inflammation associated with toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality english lavender flowers in the morning for optimal potency and freshness.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lavender flowers or two teaspoons of fresh lavender flowers per cup of boiling water.
Steep the lavender mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors and compounds to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids and sediment.
Enjoy the soothing tea warm, sweetened with honey if desired, to alleviate toothache discomfort within 10-15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol present in peppermint oil can help numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling, allowing for quicker healing. As a soothing agent, peppermint tea can also calm the nerves, providing relief from toothache-related stress and anxiety. Its gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as menthol and limonene, help kill bacteria that cause tooth infections and abscesses, reducing pain and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and teeth, providing relief from toothache pain.

Anesthetic properties: Menthol, a key compound in peppermint tea, has an anesthetic effect that can numb the affected area, temporarily relieving toothache pain and making it easier to tolerate until medical treatment is available.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol, which helps to numb the pain and reduce inflammation in the mouth, providing relief from toothaches.
Barks: The barks contain compounds like limonene and linalool, which have antibacterial properties that help fight off infection and reduce inflammation around the affected tooth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in the morning when dewy for optimal potency and flavor.
Rinse one teaspoon of dried or three teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves with cold water to remove impurities.
Steep the peppermint leaves in one cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids after ten to fifteen minutes.
Drink the cooled herbal peppermint tea within thirty minutes to an hour of preparation for maximum toothache relief benefits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. The antioxidant-rich compounds in sage also help to combat bacterial infections that can cause toothaches. Furthermore, the warm and soothing nature of the tea can provide relief by relaxing the jaw and mouth muscles, offering a calming sensation that helps to alleviate discomfort. This natural remedy is an effective and gentle way to find temporary reprieve from toothache pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums and mouth, providing relief from toothache discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: Sage tea's antimicrobial properties, including salvenside, inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause tooth decay, gum disease, and other oral infections, helping to alleviate toothache symptoms.

Soothing and analgesic property: Sage tea's flavonoids, such as quercetin, have a calming effect on the mucous membranes in the mouth, reducing inflammation and providing temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and alleviate toothache pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of sage contain salvesins, which have been shown to numb the affected area and provide relief from toothache discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves, ensuring they are free from contamination and pesticides.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the sage leaves in a heat-resistant glass teapot or mug.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the sage essential oils to infuse into the water, releasing their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming it slowly, savoring the bitter yet earthy flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric teas helps with toothache because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The active compound curcumin in turmeric tea has natural antibacterial qualities, which combat bacteria that cause infections and inflame the gums and teeth. Additionally, turmeric's antiseptic properties help soothe irritated tissues, calming toothaches caused by cavity or gum disease. As a result, drinking turmeric tea can provide instant relief from tooth pain, promoting a healthy and comfortable mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound curcumin reduces swelling and inflammation, which can alleviate toothache pain caused by gum or dental issues.
Antioxidant properties: Turmeric's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause oral infections and tissue damage, promoting healing and reducing the severity of toothaches.
Analogues to NSAIDs: Curcumin has been shown to have similar analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects as non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), providing relief from toothache pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce swelling and combat infection in the gums and teeth.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain a compound called curcumin, which has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in relieving toothache pain and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried turmeric root powder and two cups of boiling water.
Steep the turmeric powder in the hot water for five minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea to enhance its flavor and antibacterial properties.
Let the tea cool to room temperature before applying it to the affected tooth as a rinse.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The compounds found in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain by numbing the affected area. When consumed as a tea, these compounds can be absorbed into the bloodstream, reaching the affected tooth and gums to provide relief from throbbing pain and discomfort. Additionally, thyme's antibacterial properties help combat infection-causing bacteria, promoting healthy oral tissues and a speedy recovery.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial infections that may be causing toothaches, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort and pressure.
Sedative properties: Thyme's sedative properties can help calm nerves and alleviate anxiety caused by toothache pain, making it easier to relax and recover.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used to make a tea that helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain from toothache.
Barks: Thyme barks contain tannins that help to reduce bleeding and stop infection, making them an effective addition to toothache teas.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Allow the measured amount of dried thyme to steep in hot water for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the warm tea for flavor and to help soothe the toothache.
Drink the entire serving of thyme tea slowly, taking small sips every few minutes as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock teas helps with toothache because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and teeth. The tea's antiseptic properties also help combat bacteria that can cause infection, promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, burdock root has natural analgesic properties that numb the affected area, providing relief from sharp pains and sensitivity. As a result, drinking burdock teas may offer a natural and effective way to alleviate toothache discomfort and promote healing in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tea's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain caused by toothaches, making it a natural remedy for relieving discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties in burdock tea can combat bacterial infections that may be causing the toothache, helping to prevent further infection and promoting healing.

Soothing: Burdock tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the mucous membranes, which can help reduce sensitivity and discomfort caused by toothaches, making it an effective natural pain reliever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm toothaches by reducing inflammation and bacteria growth.
Barks: The bark of the burdock plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help relieve toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried burdock root and fresh ginger roots in equal proportions.
Boil one cup of water for five minutes to prepare the decoction base.
Add one teaspoon of dried burdock root and half a teaspoon of fresh ginger to the boiling water.
Simmer the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow flavors and properties to meld together.
Strain and let the tea cool before using it as a mouthwash or gargle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with toothache because its active compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When brewed into a tea, these compounds can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the affected tooth or gum area. Additionally, the antioxidants present in dandelion tea may also help combat infections that can contribute to toothache. The tea's ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues makes it an effective natural remedy for relieving toothache symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Dandelion tea's antibacterial compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, help combat bacterial infections in the mouth and throat, reducing inflammation and pain associated with toothaches.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tea, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissue, providing relief from toothache discomfort.

Astringent properties: Dandelion tea's astringent nature helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can help stop the spread of infection and promote healing in cases of gum disease or mouth sores that may be contributing to toothache.
remedy_parts: Roots: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, dandelion roots help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with toothaches.
Seeds: Containing a compound called taraxasterol, dandelion seeds have analgesic and antibacterial properties that can help soothe tooth pain and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh dandelion roots or leaves in the morning on a sunny day.
Rinse the harvested parts with cold water and pat them dry with a clean cloth gently.
Boil one quart of filtered water and add one teaspoon of dried dandelion root or two teaspoons of fresh dandelion leaves for ten minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup within five minutes.
Allow the tea to cool down for at least 30 minutes before drinking it slowly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree teas helps with toothache because it contains compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's ability to reduce swelling and alleviate pain makes it an effective natural remedy for relieving toothaches. Additionally, the antimicrobial agents in the tea help combat the underlying infection or irritation causing the toothache, promoting healing and reducing discomfort. By drinking maidenhair tree teas, individuals can experience fast and gentle relief from toothaches without the need for harsh chemicals or medication.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which could potentially help alleviate toothache pain caused by inflammation or infection.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The tea's compounds have been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi, which could help combat oral infections that may be contributing to toothache pain.

Analgesic and anti-pyretic properties: Ginkgo biloba tea has been traditionally used to treat headaches and other pains, including toothaches, possibly due to its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease pain, making them effective in alleviating toothache symptoms.
Barks: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe toothaches and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in one cup of boiling water for five minutes and let it cool down slightly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer and discard the solids immediately.
Add two drops of peppermint essential oil to enhance the flavor and aroma of the tea.
Drink the warm tea slowly and carefully, allowing it to take effect for up to an hour.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for toothache are natural remedies made from various plant-based ingredients, such as leaves, roots, and flowers, that have been traditionally used to alleviate dental pain and discomfort. These teas work by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and soothing the gums, providing relief from toothaches caused by cavities, abscesses, or gum disease. Examples of herbal teas that help with toothache include chamomile, peppermint, and sage, which can be consumed hot or cold as a mouthwash or gargle to ease pain and improve oral health. By using these teas, individuals can experience reduced discomfort and improved quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat toothache with herbal teas is to steep chamomile, lavender, or peppermint leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool before applying a damp cloth to the affected area to reduce swelling. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs help alleviate pain and discomfort, while their antibacterial properties combat infection.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal teas are gum inflammation, sore mouth, and mouth ulcers. Teas made from herbs such as sage, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated gums and mouth tissues. These teas can provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with these conditions.
